channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Firecrawl Part 2: The Confidence Gate That Decides When Bing Gets a Vote
item.description: I found the bug in the most boring place: a company record that looked "complete enough" to pass a quick glance, but still had an Unknown-ish name and no usable location. The pipeline hadn't...

item.title: AI for DevOps and Platform Engineering: Practical Use Cases That Actually Work
item.description: Moving beyond hype to real workflows where AI improves infrastructure engineering, and where AI is actually useful for DevOps and Platform Engineering teams today. INTRODUCTION AI is rapidly...

item.title: Understanding the Model Router in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Introduction As generative AI applications move from prototypes to production systems, developers increasingly face a new architectural challenge is choosing the right model for each task. Modern...

item.title: What Building Voxitale for the Gemini Live Contest Taught Me About Working With Multiple AI Tools
item.description: For the Gemini Live contest I built Voxitale, a voice-first storytelling app for young children. A child talks to a character named Amelia directly in the browser. They guide the adventure out...

item.title: Proof-of-Execution: verifying what AI agents actually execute
item.description: I’ve been working on a protocol called Proof-of-Execution (PoE). The idea is simple: AI agents today are evaluated mostly on their outputs, but outputs can be correct even if the agent didn’t...

item.title: Why Context Engineering Will Replace Traditional Architecture Thinking
item.description: Invitation: Now, I am officially active on X (Twitter). For new DevOps ideas, you can join me on X (Twitter) as well. Click Here Article Abstract: For most of the history of software engineering,...

item.title: Vite 8.0 Is Out
item.description: Article URL: https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47360730 Points: 12 # Comments: 1

item.title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
item.description: Caller identity platform Truecaller recently launched a new feature that lets one person become an admin of a family group, get alerts about fraud calls received by other members, and even end a...

item.title: How I Learned and Built AI-Integrated Software Development
item.description: The world of software development is changing rapidly. A few years ago, building applications meant writing every piece of logic manually, debugging line by line, and spending countless hours...

item.title: Systems follow what they repeat, not what you write down
item.description: Governance failures rarely start with bad policies. They start with repeated behavior. In Behavioral AI Governance, we use a simple idea to explain why systems—codebases, teams, and AI...

item.title: AI Reviews Your Code Before You Even Open the PR — Claude Code Review Changes Everything
item.description: A developer changed one line of code. Looked totally normal. The kind of change that gets approved in 30 seconds. Claude Code Review flagged it as a critical bug. That single line would have...

item.title: Build an Agent That Thinks Like a Data Scientist: How We Hit #1 on DABStep with Reusable Tool Generation
How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
item.description: GTC — which stands for GPU Technology Conference — is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically uses the spotlight to announce new products, champion partnerships, and lay out...

item.title: The best Bluetooth trackers for Apple and Android phones
item.description: Some people rarely lose things. Wallets are always exactly where they’re supposed to be, keys never go missing, and remotes never slip between the couch cushions. And then there’s the rest of us —...

item.title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
item.description: Rox, founded in 2024 by the former chief growth officer of New Relic, offers an AI-native alternative to CRM tools.

item.title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
item.description: The US military might use generative AI systems to rank lists of targets and make recommendations—which would be vetted by humans—about which to strike first, according to a Defense Department...

item.title: The who, what, and why of the attack that has shut down Stryker's Windows network
item.description: Company says it doesn't know how long it will take to restore its Microsoft environment.

item.title: Google Chrome is coming to Arm-powered Linux devices later this year
item.description: You can download Chrome for Linux, and you can download Chrome for Arm devices - but if you've got a computer running Linux on Arm, not so much! Now, Google says it's finally bringing Chrome to...

item.title: iPhones and iPads Approved for NATO Classified Data
item.description: Apple announcement: …iPhone and iPad are the first and only consumer devices in compliance with the information assurance requirements of NATO nations. This enables iPhone and iPad to be used with...

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Anthropic’s DOD Lawsuit, War Memes, and AI Coming for VC Jobs
item.description: In today’s episode, we discuss how the saga between Anthropic and the Department of Defense is far from over.

item.title: What it was like to watch grieving parents stare down Mark Zuckerberg in court
item.description: Around a dozen parents huddled in the dim hallway outside the courtroom in February, nervously gripping paper tickets. They were glaring at a gray tote bag held by a member of the court staff -...

item.title: The original AirTag is the cheapest it’s ever been
item.description: Despite the fact Apple released a new AirTag in January, the first-generation AirTag is still a top-notch tracker if you’re embedded in Apple’s ecosystem. And right now, it’s on sale for $13.91...

item.title: KPop Demon Hunters is getting a sequel, obviously
item.description: Get ready to get even more K-pop, demonier, and as huntery as possible. Netflix's smash-hit, Oscar-nominated animated film KPop Demon Hunters is returning for a sequel, with the fictional Korean...

item.title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
item.description: When buyers inquire about an item’s availability, sellers can use Meta AI to automatically draft replies using information from their listing, such as the description, availability, pickup...

item.title: Google Is Not Ruling Out Ads in Gemini
item.description: WIRED spoke with Nick Fox, Google’s SVP of knowledge and information, about how AI is changing the company’s advertising business.

item.title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
item.description: Tinder just got a major revamp as it attempts to reengage its user base and attract younger daters. This includes in-person events, AI enhancements, and even virtual speed dating.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
item.description: Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically...

item.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware...

item.title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
item.description: Atlassian laid off 10% of its workforce, around 1,600 people, as the company looks to funnel more funds to AI.

item.title: Hive0163 Uses AI-Assisted Slopoly Malware for Persistent Access in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a suspected artificial intelligence (AI)-generated malware codenamed Slopoly put to use by a financially motivated threat actor named Hive0163....
